title: rhinanthus major subsp. hirsutus
intro: Rhinanthus major subsp. hirsutus, also known as the hairy or rough-fruited broomrape, is a plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant. Rhinanthus major subsp. hirsutus can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
